relayStatus.OFFLINE: OFFLINE (Use the guide to set up the relay)
relayStatus.READY: READY (Ready to use. LMB — set as the active relay)
relayStatus.ONLINE: ONLINE (This is the active relay. LMB — disable the relay)
relayStatus.STALE: STALE (StreamKit signal was lost. LMB — disable the relay)

discordMenu.headerWarning: Temporary: if the GM minimizes the FoundryVTT window for more than a minute, the relay temporarily loses connection
discordMenu.iconsTooltip: You can drag a profile from the Discord voice channel user list into the FoundryVTT user list input to link accounts

voiceChat.profileTooltip: Drag the profile into the input field in the list on the left to link it to a FoundryVTT user

items.1: The relay must be active for at least 1 user. That user must be selected as the "Active relay" — you can do this in the user list on the Relay tab.
items.2: The relay can be started only in a browser. Even if you open the overlay window in the FoundryVTT desktop app, it will not work.
items.3: Temporary issue: if the GM (world host) minimizes the FoundryVTT window for more than a minute, all relay players temporarily lose connection.

items.1: Relay: a script that runs in a browser and forwards events from a Discord voice channel to FoundryVTT
items.2: Relay player: a player who started the relay

prep.title: Preparing and activating the relay
prep.intro: All further actions must be performed by the relay player.

items.1: Install the TamperMonkey extension in your browser and enable it
items.2: After installation, open your browser's extension settings, enable "Developer mode", and configure TamperMonkey to allow user scripts on streamkit.discord.com:
items.3: Next, open the TamperMonkey Dashboard. Usually you can open it from the extensions menu in the top-right corner of your browser.
items.4: In the Dashboard, find the [+] button to add a script. Usually it is above the script list, next to "Installed Scripts".
items.5Prefix: A script code editor will open. Copy the script below and paste it here, then save the script (
items.6: Return to the script Dashboard. A script called "Streamkit Speaking Relay" should appear in the script list. Enable it if it is not already enabled.

browsers.chrome: Chrome: "Details" → "Site access", choose "On all sites" (Or "On specific sites" and enter "https://streamkit.discord.com")

edgeItems.1: "Details" → "Site access", choose "On all sites" (Or "On specific sites" and enter "https://streamkit.discord.com")

items.1: Join the FoundryVTT world as any user via a browser and open the Discord Streamkit overlay window.
items.1a: You can do this via the FoundryVTT settings menu, the button in the VisualNovelDialogues Theater, or the console command DiscordBridge.openMenu().
items.2: Enter the link to the Discord voice channel you need into the "Voice channel link" field and enable the checkbox below.
items.2a: You can get a Discord voice channel link by RMB-clicking it and selecting "Copy Link". After pasting, the link will be converted to SERVER_ID/CHANNEL_ID format automatically.
items.2b: Alternatively, if Discord Developer Mode is enabled, you can manually copy the server ID and voice channel ID and enter them as SERVER_ID/CHANNEL_ID.
items.4: Now, if everything is OK, all FoundryVTT users will see the Discord voice channel and all of its participants.

items.1: You must be in this voice channel to receive the "Start speaking" and "Stop speaking" events
items.2: All events ("start/stop speaking", "user connected/disconnected", etc.) will be sent to FoundryVTT via hooks, which you can use in macros and other modules.
items.3: When you close the Discord Streamkit overlay window, the relay will lose signal and will be marked as STALE
items.4: The VisualNovelDialogues module automatically receives relay events. You can configure how VisualNovelDialogues handles relay events in its settings.

errors.listNoStatus: Error: status is unknown. Try reopening the window
